Code fragment: public final TableField<VideoRecord, Integer> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key is named just 'id' which is generic and doesn't convey meaning about the entity. Consider renaming it to 'video_id' to make it more descriptive and self-documenting when used in joins or foreign key references.

Code fragment: public final TableField<VideoRecord, LocalDateTime> CREATED_AT = createField(DSL.name("created_at"), SQLDataType.LOCALDATETIME(6).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("now()"), SQLDataType.LOCALDATETIME)), this, "");
Explanation: The 'created_at' column has a default value but is still marked as NULLABLE (no .nullable(false) specified), which means it can never be NULL in practice but the schema allows it. Add .nullable(false) to the column definition to enforce the NOT NULL constraint.

Using selectFrom(VIDEO) fetches all columns from the VIDEO table, which may retrieve unnecessary data like large TRANSCRIPTION or SUMMARY fields. Consider using select() with only the specific columns needed for this operation.
